author: @revoxjazz
text:
The great truth of the question that we all must ask when we intend to make a better world, exactly as you said ...: "what can you do with what there is around you".
Honestly, in your case and with the elementary conditions that you have in your workshop maybe it would be good if you added more things like this. I bet there's a lot going on that can be spectacular at the bottom of those cabinets and tables .... Just my opinion, Robert.
Cheers!

author: @supertom7908
text:
Sir, I’m sorry, I missed a few classes and am still playing catch-up. No sick note. The doctor insisted on writing CV something on it.

The pace of the class is normally fast and I prefer it that way.

I missed the end of the Rotating Ventilator Research Project. (I hear that you are stubborn. Good. Hint: Rotating Ventilator + Microwave Oven Transformer Generator. Bob’s my brother.)

I had no idea that you’ve switched to warp speed In my absence though. 
One moment we where generating electricity the next we doing gynecology. 

All I see are two ovaries and that other part or is there something wrong with me?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you see what you see mate - that's art lol - the rotating generator is on it's own playlist and all the parts are there if you need to catch up

author: @your_utube
text: Robert there you go yet again touching on one of my interests. Having grown up with a father who is very artful. I was exposed to art forms in various media, from painting to woodwork, mecrame, weaving, basket-making, paper mache, clay sculpture, metal work etc. One of the things I found fascinating is my dad's ability to see art in mundane things that on the face of it appeared to be rather ordinary and unartful. Thanks for this foray into the very useful world of art. It brings to the fore the issue of "form vs function", and hitherto we have been mostly into the function bit. Very interesting indeed! So delightful!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: everyone should try a little 'art' as a lesson in creativity mate - one door always opens on at least 6 others lol

author: @jonnyswalk_7
text:
I really enjoy the diversity of topics and ideas that you cover on here Robert.
They invariably promote really creative,encouraging & positive uses of thought and energies. 
We find ourselves in the midst of a very difficult time with a raft of substantial challenges for the whole human race including the physical dangers of the pandemic but also increasingly the genuine psychological and emotional threats  to “Joe average’s” (I am he) state of mental health and well being. This pandemic in my opinion threatens much more than our physical health

Amidst that we seek out ways of avoiding thinking too much about it all.
There are many different avenues of entertainment TV being the mainstay of many,but in my opinion a lot (not all obviously) can be mindless (which is sometimes the idea) and emptying  - I personally find a lot “TV” of very little to no therapeutic value.
And watching the news(though I realise that you have to keep somewhat abreast of what’s going on) outright depressing and even distressing (my sympathies go out to all those affected of course)
So to get away from when I’m unable to get out walking the dog or I cant sleep I’ve turned to a little You Tube when a couple of months ago I came across your channel.
I was I admit very daunted and honestly a little confused by some of the technical speak (though that’s the fault of my education not your vocabulary)
Now I guess I may be one of the less (as in non) active “engineer type”viewers of your channel so I’ll not be able to share any of my homemade Robert Murray-Smith creations any time soon - My excuses are as follows lol : I don’t and may never have even a modest a workshop or space. I’d not have the room physically or mentally if I’m honest to accomplish some of your experiments or now art 🖼 but who knows what the future holds.
But in my case thats not the point - I am I suppose just a simple passive watcher (I am about as practical as a china trowel also ) but I can attest to the positive and uplifting effect that watching your experiments,creations and how-to’s (in my own limited way) has had.
It has expanded my horizons somewhat and just watching someone doing a good creative and positive thing - in this case using stuff thats often thrown away and engineer it into useful and interesting pieces of functioning kit or templates for ideas etc,is again wonderfully positive, and uplifting amidst the dark backdrop of the current days.
And of course your personality shines through so well - I’ve also enjoyed your thoughts and reflections during your “talky” vids also 
To summarise then and In simple speak, I find the channel and yourself, just “good wholesome stuff” - and that’s not as common as it once was these days
And now you’ve branched into art - like a modern day Da Vinci!!
So Thank you Robert. 
I’ve no doubt that a lot of time and effort goes into all this so again many thanks 😊 👍 
Warmest wishes from South Wales 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 - Jonathan 😊 👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Hiya mate - first off - thank you for taking the time to write that post and share your thoughts - it is quite simply a beautiful world and a joy to be alive in it - yes we have problems but we also hold the key to the solutions and mostly that key concerns our own mental attitude - it's never what it is but always how you look at it - all the best, Rob

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering 😎👍

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 

"we all see what we see mate - it's funny how people all view things differently"


Yes but on occasion I tend to take it to the extreme.

One time years ago while visualizing a design for something, (when i say visualize I mean, it's like virtual reality without the computer and goggles).

In any case, I needed to see the other side of the object and couldn't get it to turn just by thinking, got a bit frustrated and reached up, grabbed the object and turned it back and forth.

The guy next to me said: "What are you doing?"

I said: "I'm designing something and I needed to see the other side so I turned it."

He walked away. 

I can't blame him too much; as far as he could tell, I was turning nothing but air. 😂🤣😂

author: @ianmcevoy7106

text: But MY found art I bought online is 16% more efficient.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890

replies.text: Steve Tobias okay, 15% more efficient.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @twestgard2 please explain your definition of efficiency based on something made from object salvaged for free costing only time compared to something purchased

replies.author: @twestgard2
replies.text: Steve Tobias oh Lordy, you are sooo missing the point here.

replies.author: @twestgard2
replies.text: The joke here is that people keep telling RMS that his inventions aren’t efficient, when he never claimed they were and in fact has been crystal clear all along that efficiency wasn’t one of his design criteria. (Other than the efficiency of “look what I found”).
